title: www_justice_gov_uk_courts_procedure_rules_civil_contents_frontmatter_Notes23_342fc43e3d.html
content: Notes to Accompany May 2001 23rd Supplement – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter Notes to Accompany May 2001 23rd Supplement Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » Notes to Accompany May 2001 23rd Supplement Menu ≡ Notes to Accompany May 2001 23rd Supplement Amendments to existing material are included as follows: Rules THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Part 6 Rule 6.18 new sub paragraph inserted (defining the EC Service Regulation) Rule 6.20(18) ‘is’ inserted after ‘claim’ Rule 6.24(1) in sub-paragraph (b)(i) ‘or’; deleted sub-paragraph (b)(ii) shall stand as sub-paragraph (b)(iii); new text inserted before sub-paragraph (b)(iii) Rule 6.25 paragraph (4) at the end of sub-paragraph (b), ‘or’ inserted , in sub-paragraph (c) for ‘; or’ ‘.’ substituted; and sub-paragraph (b) omitted New text inserted after paragraph 4. Rule 6.26 new text inserted after paragraph 6; new text inserted after Rule 6.26 (New Rule 6.26A) Part 12 Rule 12.3 new paragraph inserted after the cross-reference at the end of the rule. Part 13 Rule 13.3 new text inserted after the cross-reference. Part 22 Rule 22.1 paragraph 1 in sub paragraph (c) ‘and’ deleted; and at the end of sub-paragraph (d) new text inserted (containing sub-paragraph (e)) Part 39 After rule 39.7 new paragraph 39.8 inserted. Part 55 New Part 55 inserted. NOTE: These rules do not come into force until 15 October 2001 Part 56 New Part 56 inserted. NOTE: These rules do not come into force until 15 October 2001 Part 57 New Part 57 inserted. NOTE: These rules do not come into force until 15 October 2001 Note: Where a claim form – relates to proceedings to which Part 55, Part 56 or Part 57 would apply if it was issued on or after 15th October 2001; but is issued before that date, those rules shall not apply, and the rules of court in force immediately before that date shall apply as if they had not been amended or revoked RSC Order 115 After rule 23, heading of Section III changed to read: III: TERRORISM ACT 2000(1). Rule 24 para (a) –‘Prevention of Terrorism … Act 1989’ replaced with ‘Terrorism Act 2000’. At the end of para (b), ‘and’ deleted. After para (b) new para (ba) inserted. At the beginning of para (c), ‘other’ inserted; In para(c), ‘Part III of, and Schedule 4 to’ substituted for ‘Schedule 4 to’. In rule 26(1), ‘paragraphs 3 & 4’ replaced with ‘paragraph 5’. In rule 26(2), ‘Part III’ replaced with ‘any of sections 15 to 18’. In rule 27(2), new text inserted. In rule 29, ‘para 7 of Schedule 4’ replaced with ‘paragraph 9 or 10 of Schedule 4’ and ‘‘the relevant authority under para 7(5) replaced with ‘the person or body by whom compensation, if ordered, will be payable under paragraph 9(6) or 10(4)’. In rule 32(1), ‘Crown Office’ replaced with ‘Administrative Court’. In rule 35(3) ‘para 5 or 6‘ replaced with ‘para 7 or 8’ and para 9(6) replaced with para 13(6). CCR Order 48B Title replaced with ‘Enforcement of traffic penalties’ ‘parking enforcement centre’, wherever it appears, replaced with ‘traffic enforcement centre’ In rule 1(1), sub-para (a) ‘and’ deleted and At the end of sub-para (b) new paragraph (c) inserted Rule 1(2), definition of ‘order’ and ‘relevant period’ replaced Rule 1(2) in the definition of ‘specified debts’, ‘Part II’ deleted Rule 1(2) in the definition of ‘statutory declaration’ new text inserted and ‘and’ deleted At the end of the definition of ‘the 1991 Act’ ‘and inserted; ‘the 1996 Act means the London Local Authorities Act 1996’ In sub-para (a) of rule 1(4), after ‘London authority’, new text inserted In sub-para (c) of rule 2(3) ‘the parking attendant who issued the penalty charge notice believed’ replaced with ‘it is claimed that’ In rule 4, para (1), after ‘the 1991 Act’, ‘and paras 10(4) and (5)(d) of Schedule 1 to the 1996 Act’ inserted and In para (2), after ‘the 1991 Act’, ‘or para 10(4) of the Schedule 1 to the 1996 Act’ inserted. In rule 5(7) new text inserted after ‘Schedule 6 to the 1991 Act, and in sub-paragraph (c). THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK CCR Order 48D ‘parking enforcement centre’, wherever it occurs, replaced with ‘traffic enforcement centre’. In rule 1(2), in the definition of ‘specified debts’, ‘Part II’ deleted. Practice Directions Before Part 1 New note on practice directions inserted immediately before Part 1. Part 2B Paragraph 8.3 reference (in error) to section 18 (of the County Courts Act) amended to section 118. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Part 6B New text inserted before existing para 1.1 ‘Republic of Ireland’ deleted from title of para 3.1 EU Service Regulation appended to Practice Direction THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Part 7C New para 1.3(2)(e) substituted for existing para New para 5.2(4) substituted for existing para. Part 33 New Practice Direction inserted on ‘Land Registration Act’ Part 39B New Practice Direction on ‘Court Sittings in 2002’ inserted THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Part 39C New Practice Direction inserted on ‘Claims under the Race Relations Act 1976 (National Security) Part 44 Para. 17.5 ‘court officer’ amended to ‘costs officer’ Part 49G New para 2.1A inserted after para 2.1 New para 10.3A inserted after para 10.3 New para 12 substituted for existing para Part 55 New practice direction inserted on Housing Possession Note: this PD does not come into force until 15 October 2001 Part 56 New practice direction inserted on Housing (Landlord & Tenant) Note: this PD does not come into force until 15 October 2001 Part 57 New practice direction inserted on Probate Note: this PD does not come into force until 15 October 2001 Note: Where a claim form – relates to proceedings to which Part 55, Part 56 or Part 57 would apply if it was issued on or after 15th October 2001; but is issued before that date, those rules shall not apply, and the rules of court in force immediately before that date shall apply as if they had not been amended or revoked CCR Order 48B Existing Practice Direction replaced with amended version Other Practice Directions Insolvency Proceedings New sub-para 15.10 inserted after 15.9 Director’s Disqualification Error in para 11.6 ‘Register’ amended to ‘Registrar’ 2 new forms inserted at the end. Pre-Action Protocols Clinical Disputes Job title address and telephone number of Dr Alastair Scotland amended Professional Negligence New protocol added at end of existing protocols THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Protocols Practice Direction Reference to new Professional Negligence protocol inserted in table at para 5.1 Forms The following is a list of new or revised forms included with this edition but which will not come into force until 15 October 2001 N206B Notice of issue (possession claim) a new form which does not replace the N206. N5 Claim form for possession of property – a new single claim form to be used for starting a claim in all possession cases, except forfeiture N5A Claim form for relief against forfeiture – new form N7 Notes for defendant (mortgaged residential premises) N7A Notes for defendant (rented residential premises) N7B Notes for defendant (forfeiture of the lease) N119 Particulars of claim (rented residential premises) – revised version of existing form N120 Particulars of claim (mortgaged residential premises) – revised version of existing form N121 Particulars of claim (trespassers) – new form N119A Notes for guidance on completing particulars of claim (rented residential premises) N11 Form of defence (Part 55 claims) – revised version of existing form N11R Form of defence (rented residential premises) – revised version of existing form N11M Form of defence (mortgaged residential premises) – revised version of existing form N206A Notice of issue – revised version of existing form N5B Claim form for possession (accelerated procedure) – revised version of existing form N5C Notes for the claimant (APP) N11B Defence Form (APP) – revised version of existing form COUNTY COURT FORMS WHICH BECOME OBSOLETE FROM 15 OCTOBER N8 Notice to the respondent N8(1) Notice to respondent when a matter will be heard under Order 24 N8(2) Notice to respondent in an application under Section 53 of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 N8(4) Notice to respondent in an application under Section 17 or 18 of the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 N27(1) Judgment for claimant (forfeiture) order refused under the Rent Acts N29 Order for possession (mortgage possession) N36 Order for possession (under Order 24) N220 Request for the service of a possession summons on the defendant’s representative N220(1) Notice to the representative of a defendant in an action for the recovery of land N229 Notice of admission in a possession action N291 Judgment for a defendant in action for the recovery of land N312 (now N208–N210) Originating application for determining the right to possession (Landlord and Tenant Act 1927) N394 Originating application for determining the right to compensation (Landlord and Tenant Act 1927) N395 Originating application of a certificate or determination of a question other than compensation (Landlord and Tenant Act 1927) N396 Originating application for statutory tenancy under Part I of LTA 1954 N397 Originating application for new tenancy under Part II of LTA 1954 N398 Order on application for certificate as to improvement (LTA 1954) N399 Final Order (LTA 1954) N400 Answer to originating application for new tenancy under Part 1 LTA 1954 N401 Answer to originating application for new tenancy under Part II LTA 1954 N402 Order substituting date in landlord’s notice or tenant’s request N403 Application for certificate of ground for refusing new tenancy N404 Certificate of ground for refusing new tenancy N404(1) Order authorising agreement excluding act N405 Notice of proceedings N406 Notice of day on which special case will be heard (Agricultural Holdings Act 1986) N407 Order on hearing of special case N425 Notice to repair N426 Notice of proceedings HIGH COURT FORMS WHICH BECOME OBSOLETE FROM 15 OCTOBER No 42 Default judgment in an action for the possession of land No 42A Order for possession under Order 113 No 11A Originating summons for possession under Order 113 PF5 Order as to service in an action for possession PF9 Summons for judgment for possession PF79 Judgment in default in an action for the possession of land PF91 Form of affidavit in support of an application to enforce a judgment for possession. Coming into Force Rules The above rule changes come into force on 31 May 2001, with the following exceptions: Amendments to CCR Order 48B and CCR Order 48D on 1 June 2001 Parts 55, 56 & 57 on 15 October 2001 Practice directions The above practice direction changes come into force on 31 May 2001 with the following exceptions: Amendment to PD 48B on 1 June 2001 New PDs for Parts 55,56 & 57 on 15 October 2001 Forms All Forms relating to new Part 55 come into force on 15 October 2001. It is not proposed to produce additional forms in respect of Part 56.
